title: 3,000+ Tokens Per Second? Let's Stress Test Xiaomi's New Model: MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed
summary: Xiaomi and TileRT released MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed, a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model achieving over 1,000 tokens per second on standard hardware. The model uses selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a TileRT persistent GPU kernel runtime to optimize inference speed.
writeup: The video stress tests MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed, a 1-trillion-parameter Mixture-of-Experts model from Xiaomi and TileRT that reaches over 1,000 tokens per second on standard hardware. It examines the three techniques behind that throughput: selective FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a TileRT persistent GPU kernel runtime. The takeaway is that inference speed gains come from combining sparse MoE routing with reduced-precision weights, draft-based decoding, and a kernel runtime tuned to keep the GPU busy.
